dal_dashboard_notes: Rank 1 of 58 | Tier 1 — Core Driver | Process KPI | Strongest predictor of winning in AUS. Measures how efficiently the team converts expected-goal chances into goals.
Rank 2 of 58 | Tier 1 — Core Driver | Process KPI | Even-strength shot-attempt share. Best proxy for territorial control and sustained offensive pressure at 5-on-5.
Rank 3 of 58 | Tier 1 — Core Driver | Process KPI | High-danger shot volume. Directly linked to goal probability. Tier 1 offensive process driver.
Rank 4 of 58 | Tier 1 — Core Driver | Process KPI | On-net shot volume. Consistent Tier 1 predictor of offensive activity and pressure generation.
Rank 5 of 58 | Tier 1 — Core Driver | Process KPI | xG differential. Best combined on-ice metric — accounts for both offensive creation and defensive concession.
Rank 6 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Total shot attempts. Strong proxy for offensive puck possession and sustained territorial pressure.
Rank 7 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Quality and volume of scoring chances allowed. Primary defensive process measure. Lower = better.
Rank 8 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Cumulative expected goals generated. Volume of quality chance creation — closely paired with xG conversion.
Rank 9 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Power play goals scored. Most direct measure of man-advantage execution.
Rank 10 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | OZ possession sequences that produce a shot. Highest-quality measure of productive offensive zone time.
Rank 11 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | PP conversion rate. Efficiency of man-advantage situations. Strong special teams predictor.
Rank 12 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Total zone entries. Drives offensive possession — more entries means more shots and more OZ time.
Rank 13 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Passes delivered into the high-danger slot area. Key pre-shot movement indicator. Dal priority development area.
Rank 14 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Total offensive zone possession time. Broad measure of sustained forward pressure.
Rank 15 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Offensive zone possession sequences. Correlated with controlled zone time and shot generation.
Rank 16 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Even-strength puck retrievals in own zone. Higher count signals prolonged defensive zone time. Lower = better.
Rank 17 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Even-strength puck retrievals in offensive zone. Reflects ability to sustain pressure and win puck battles up ice.
Rank 18 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Off-target shot volume. Reflects overall shooting activity and offensive zone presence.
Rank 19 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | PK success rate. Strong predictor of special teams defensive performance.
Rank 20 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Neutral zone turnovers. Interrupts transition and generates direct counter-attacks. Dal priority defensive gap.
Rank 21 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Passes completed immediately before a shot. Measures pre-shot movement quality — reflects shot setup.
Rank 22 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Total defensive zone possession time. Lower = less time under pressure in own end.
Rank 23 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Pass completion rate. Reflects puck management quality and execution under pressure.
Rank 24 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Shot accuracy rate (shots on goal ÷ total shots). Reflects shot quality and net presence.
Rank 25 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Turnovers in own defensive zone. Creates immediate high-danger scoring opportunities for opponents.
Rank 26 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Puck battle events in own zone. Fewer DZ battles = less time under defensive pressure.
Rank 27 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Rate of puck battle success across all zones. Reflects compete level at board battles.
Rank 28 of 58 | Tier 2 — Important Driver | Process KPI | Offensive zone possession time. Broader territorial control measure — overlaps with OZ play.
Rank 29 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Process KPI | Steals by the team in own defensive zone. High count signals prolonged DZ pressure.
Rank 30 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Defensive zone possession time. Lower = less time under pressure in own end.
Rank 31 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | xG required per goal scored. Lower = more clinical finishing relative to shot quality generated.
Rank 32 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Zone exit events from own end. Fewer breakouts needed signals less time defending own zone.
Rank 33 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | xG opponent requires per goal scored against Dal. Higher = opponents shooting inefficiently.

Rank 34 of 58 | Tier 1 — Core Driver | Outcome/Context | Outcome metric. Scoring is the objective of offensive play, but kept low-weighted so process indicators — xG, scoring chances, OZ possession — remain dominant.
Rank 35 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Average xG value of each opponent shot allowed. Lower = better shot suppression and defensive structure.
Rank 36 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Average shot quality generated. Higher = better shot selection and chance quality.
Rank 37 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Neutral zone puck battles. Lower count may reflect cleaner transition play.
Rank 38 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Overall faceoff win rate. Provides clean possession starts. Rate version only — counts excluded.
Rank 39 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | DZ faceoff win rate. Reduces immediate pressure in own zone following icing or goalie freezes.
Rank 40 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Neutral zone possession time. Proxy for transition control — teams that own the NZ control game flow.
Rank 41 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Dal's shots blocked by opponents. Fewer = better shot lane creation.
Rank 42 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Shots blocked by Dal defenders. Reflects shot-blocking effort and defensive commitment.
Rank 43 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Puck retrievals while on the penalty kill. Lower preferred — signals cleaner PK execution.
Rank 44 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | NZ faceoff win rate. Influences transition possession directly from neutral zone draws.
Rank 45 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | OZ faceoff win rate. Maintains offensive zone possession after stoppages.
Rank 46 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Rate at which counter-attacks produce a shot. Reflects transition efficiency and quick-strike ability.
Rank 47 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Puck battle events in offensive zone. More OZ battles = more sustained offensive zone pressure.
Rank 48 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Total ice time while shorthanded. Better PK burden measure than count alone — captures major penalties and extended kills.
Rank 49 of 58 | Tier 3 — Supporting Indicator | Supporting Context | Total penalty minutes taken. Lower = better discipline and fewer PK situations faced.
Rank 50 of 58 | Tier 4 — Context Metric | Supporting Context | Counter-attacks that generate a shot. Reflects transition-to-shot conversion.
Rank 51 of 58 | Tier 4 — Context Metric | Supporting Context | Dump-in zone entries. Paired with Entries — allows calculation of controlled entry rate.
Rank 52 of 58 | Tier 4 — Context Metric | Supporting Context | Steals in offensive zone. Generates immediate high-danger chances. Dal development priority.
Rank 53 of 58 | Tier 4 — Context Metric | Supporting Context | Percentage of OZ sequences that include a shot. Secondary efficiency context — overlaps with OZ play with shots.
Rank 54 of 58 | Tier 4 — Context Metric | Supporting Context | Total counter-attack sequences. Volume context — paired with counter-attack shot % for effectiveness.
Rank 55 of 58 | Tier 4 — Context Metric | Supporting Context | Turnovers in offensive zone. Near-noise (|r|=0.02). Retained for zone-level completeness.
Rank 56 of 58 | Tier 4 — Context Metric | Supporting Context | Puck retrievals during power plays. Lower preferred — signals cleaner PP execution.
Rank 57 of 58 | Tier 4 — Context Metric | Supporting Context | Neutral zone steals. Minimal predictive power (|r|=0.01). Retained for zone-level completeness.
Rank 58 of 58 | Tier 4 — Context Metric | Supporting Context | Dump-out exits from own zone. Context dependent — can reflect smart zone exits or passive defending.
